bids.reasoning: I am targeting maximum utility at the opening because time pressure is nonexistent at t=0.00, and there is no reason to concede yet. The opponent’s offer is valid but gives me my lowest value on every issue, and their threat to walk away is cheap talk unsupported by any bidding history.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession from my prior all-valueA offer to avoid simply repeating it. The opponent has held firm at the lowest-utility bid for me and their messages are threats rather than evidence of real concession, so I will concede only on my least important issue, issueD.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making a small additional concession on the lowest-weight issue. The opponent has only moved slightly from all valueF to issueA:valueE with the rest still valueF, so their offers remain very low utility to me; I treat their threats as cheap talk rather than evidence of a real deadline.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.95 because it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only moved slightly from all valueF to issueA:valueE while using threats, so I treat the messages as pressure tactics and concede only on my lowest-weight issueD for now.

I am targeting a high-utility early-game bid, since t=0.08 leaves plenty of time and the opponent’s offers are still very low for me despite slight movement. Their messages are aggressive threats, but the actual bidding shows only small concessions, so I will concede only on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on higher-weight issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while showing continued movement on my least important issue. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me, with the latest still quite low, and their threats are not credible enough to justify major concessions this early. I will hold firm on high-weight issues A, B, and C while fully conceding issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.89 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers yet. The opponent has made only small concessions and their threats to walk are cheap talk; however, I will continue moving on lower-weight issues first while holding firm on the most important issues A and B.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.87 because it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.14), so there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s bids have only slightly improved for me and their threats are cheap talk, but I will avoid repeating my last exact bid by making a modest concession on issueB while holding firm on high-value outcomes elsewhere.

I am targeting a high but slightly more concessive utility around 0.856, since it is still early at t=0.16 and there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s offers have improved from all valueF to a latest offer worth about 0.392 to me, so they are conceding somewhat despite their aggressive messages. I am conceding further on lower-weight issues C and D while holding firm on the higher-weight issues A and B.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.82 because it is still early in the negotiation, so there is no need to match the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent’s messages are mostly threats and urgency tactics, while their actual offers show only limited and inconsistent concession, with the best signal so far being some movement on issueA; I will keep issueA and issueB firm and concede further on lower-weight issueC/issueD.

At t=0.20 I am still targeting a strong utility around 0.86, since there is substantial time left and the opponent’s threats are cheap talk. Their latest valid offer improves issueA to valueB but keeps the other issues at valueF, so I am reciprocating on issueA while holding firmer on the higher-value issueB and issueC and conceding the lowest-weight issueD.

I targeted a high utility around 0.84 because it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.22, so there is no need to drop close to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s actual bids show meaningful movement on issueA up to my best value, while their threats are cheap talk; I therefore hold issueA firm and make concessions on lower-priority issueD plus moderate concessions on issueB and issueC.

I targeted a still-strong utility around 0.81 because it is early-mid negotiation at t=0.24, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low-utility latest offer. The opponent’s messages are mostly threats and urgency tactics, while the actual offers show only limited and inconsistent concession; I will hold firm on high-weight issueA while conceding further on lower-weight issueC and already-low issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 because it is only t=0.26, so there is no need to collapse toward the opponent’s low offers yet. The opponent’s numeric bids have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueA and issueB, despite repeated walk-away threats; I treat those messages as pressure tactics and concede mainly on lower-weight issueC/issueD while protecting issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues C and D while preserving high value on issueA and a reasonable position on issueB. At t=0.28 there is some pressure to move beyond my earlier counters, but it is not late enough to accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers; their threats are cheap talk, and their actual bids show only limited, inconsistent concession.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, conceding meaningfully but still protecting the highest-weight issueA. The opponent’s offers and threats show some movement on issueA but continued pressure for low values on issueB/issueC/issueD; at t=0.30 there is time left, so I will concede mainly on lower and mid-priority issues without accepting their lopsided proposal.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69–0.70 because the negotiation is only at t=0.32, so there is no need to drop near the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent’s bids have mostly kept issueB/issueC/issueD near valueF while occasionally conceding on issueA, and their threatening messages are cheap talk, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA while conceding further on lower-weight issueC and already conceding issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68: a meaningful concession from my prior offer while still protecting the highest-weight issues. The opponent’s threats to walk are cheap talk at t=0.34, but their offers show some movement on issueA and issueB, so I am combining concessions they have already signaled while giving ground on lower-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.65, conceding further from my prior offer on lower-weight issueC while holding stronger positions on the higher-weight issues A and B. At t=0.36 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s latest low-utility bid, but their offers show movement especially on issueA and issueB; their repeated walk-away threats are cheap talk and not decisive. This bid uses only valid issues/values and moves closer to combinations the opponent has signaled, particularly issueB=valueB and low concessions on C/D.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.62, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier offers while still protecting my highest-weight issueA. The opponent’s messages are mostly threats and urgency claims, so I rely on the offers instead: they have shown some flexibility on issueA and issueB, but continue pushing hard on issueC/issueD, so I concede more there and make a compromise on issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.65, preserving value on the high-weight issueA while conceding substantially on issueC and issueD. At t=0.40 we are in mid-game, so I should move toward the opponent’s pattern without accepting their latest lower-utility offer; their threats to walk have been repeated since the start, so I treat them as cheap talk and focus on their actual concessions, especially their recent willingness to offer issueB: valueB.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.61, conceding substantially from my earlier high-utility bids while still staying above the opponent’s latest offer value to me. At t=0.42 there is meaningful time pressure but not a deadline crisis, so I should move toward their signaled region without fully capitulating; their threats are repetitive cheap talk, but their offers show real concessions on issueA and issueB. I am holding some value on issueC, where they have previously offered valueE, while accepting their recent movement on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.58, which is a meaningful concession from my prior bids but still well above reservation value. At t=0.44 we are in the mid-game, so I should move on lower-weight issues first while protecting issueA and issueB as much as possible. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their t=0.42 bid signaled they may tolerate issueA=valueC and issueB=valueB, so I am matching that structure to improve deal chances.
